items.title: V-Mart Retail Q1 Results: Profit surges 40% to Rs 47 crore
items.summary: V-Mart Retail has reported an impressive 40.5% increase in net profit for the June quarter, reaching Rs 47.21 crore, up from Rs 33.60 crore. Revenue from operations surged by 23% to Rs 1,088.81 crore, while total expenses grew by 22% to Rs 1,031.94 crore. The company has also broadened its horizons by launching 15 new store outlets, reflecting its expansive growth strategy.

This initiative aims to reduce reliance on costly bulk deposits for the institution. First quarter net profit jumped 36% to Rs 3,068 crore, showing strong performance. Advances grew 18.6% while deposits increased 14.9% during the period.

items.title: India's SBI Card posts higher quarterly profit on asset quality boost
items.summary: SBI Cards announced an impressive profit surge attributed to lower provisions for bad loans. A remarkable rise in card spending highlighted robust consumer and corporate appetite, alongside a boost in total revenue propelled by increased transaction volumes across their card offerings. Furthermore, the company's asset quality has notably improved with a decline in gross non-performing assets and a significant yearly drop in loan loss provisions and credit costs.

items.title: Yen heads for biggest weekly drop since May despite Tokyo's support pledges
items.summary: The Japanese yen is experiencing a dramatic drop, marking its most significant weekly decline since May. The currency has hit 40-year lows against the US dollar, compounded by Japan's ineffective measures to bolster its value. Additionally, surging oil prices have reignited inflation worries, further enhancing the dollar's strength, while the euro and sterling have seen slight increases against it.

items.title: Sebi bars former Axis MF dealer Viresh Joshi from securities markets for 7 years front-running case
items.summary: Sebi has banned Viresh Joshi and Prijesh Kurani from the securities market for seven years. This action stems from a front-running case involving Axis Mutual Fund trades. The regulator also ordered Rs 30.56 crore in impounded funds to be treated as disgorgement. Joshi allegedly shared non-public fund information with Kurani for illicit trading. The scheme involved multiple individuals and entities facilitating these wrongful gains.

items.summary: Veteran investor John Rogers emphasizes the constant need to guard against groupthink. This phenomenon occurs when individuals conform to group views instead of independent evaluation. Encouraging diverse perspectives and challenging assumptions improves decision-making in various settings.

items.title: Digital Realty raises annual FFO forecast on robust data center demand

For fiscal 2026, the expected adjusted funds from operations now range between $8.15 and $8.20. Additionally, the company upped its annual revenue projections to between $6.85 billion and $6.

items.title: KFC India operator bets on first-time customers as ₹99 meals drive turnaround
items.summary: Sapphire Foods is shifting strategy to attract new customers with value meals. The company reported a net profit in the June quarter after implementing this approach. They aim to double their consumer base to over 100 million in five years. This strategy is driving transaction growth and average daily sales for KFC India. India's organized QSR market is projected for significant expansion in the coming years.

items.title: Indonesia raises $1.03 billion in debut panda bond sale
items.summary: Indonesia made a spectacular debut in the international debt market by successfully raising $1.03 billion with its first panda bonds. The nation issued 7 billion yuan worth of these bonds, attracting overwhelming interest from investors. The three-year notes presented a yield of 1.9%, while the five-year notes reached a yield of 2.19%. The bond settlement is expected on July 30th, showcasing Indonesia's strong financial appeal.

items.title: Text message scams jump 146% in India amid rise in scam-led digital fraud: Report

items.summary: Experts in road safety are advocating for the essential development of effective and economical strategies to minimize accidents in India. They believe that targeted, evidence-based approaches can significantly diminish road fatalities, particularly in urban settings. Simple engineering enhancements, like the addition of roundabouts and better illumination, can effectively lower accident rates. These quick-to-implement solutions offer urgent safety improvements and are complemented by innovative tech that aids traffic safety measures.
